title: Create durable advanced program packets for AMP101 through AMP162
persona: Enterprise operator reviewing agent, customer, ESG, risk, process, mobile, facility, finance, legal, and learning workflows
steps: Open representative AMP101-AMP162 workspaces from the Assistant subnav.
Review source signals, risks, recommendations, artifact, approval route, and rollback controls.
expected: Each AMP101-AMP162 workspace persists an AssistantAdvancedProgramPacket row with a distinct ampNumber and programKey.
Each program has distinct title, nav label, artifact label, approval owners, and no-mutation scope.
The shared route and UI display the packet preview, historical packet list, review note, and queue-review action.

No agent, incident, tenant, connector, ESG, disruption, customer, procurement, inventory, executive reporting, process, AI, safety, facility, tax, treasury, insurance, legal, or enterprise learning records are mutated automatically.

title: Queue human review for AMP101 through AMP162 packets
steps: Create or select a DRAFT packet.
Add a review note explaining the downstream decision under consideration.
expected: The packet status changes to REVIEW_QUEUED and records actionQueueItemId, approvedByUserId, approvedAt, and approvalNote.
AssistantActionQueueItem uses an AMP-specific actionKind such as assistant_amp101_review or assistant_amp162_review.
AssistantAuditEvent states that downstream records were not changed automatically.

title: Enterprise learning and automation guardrails stay explicit
steps: Open an AMP101-AMP162 packet with active risks.
Inspect recommendationJson, approvalPlanJson, artifactJson, and rollbackPlanJson.
Queue review without executing downstream policy, prompt, model, playbook, or automation changes.
expected: Recommendations require human approval before changing production assistant behavior.
Approval plans identify named owner groups for review.
Rollback plans preserve source records and require a fresh packet when evidence materially changes.
Enterprise learning remains review-only and does not self-modify policies, prompts, playbooks, or autonomy behavior.
